name: math-format superscript/subscript regex captures only one digit
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L1-08a] result.replace(/\^(\d)/g,...) captures one digit so 10^23 renders 10-squared-3 (same for subscripts). Fix: use \d+.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: apps/mobile/src/lib/math-format.ts handles braced exponent/subscript groups with multi-character captures, but the unbraced digit regexes use /(\d)/, so x^23 becomes x squared plus literal 3 and a_12 becomes a subscript-one plus literal 2. Variants: (a) unbraced multi-digit superscripts, (b) unbraced multi-digit subscripts, (c) existing single-digit and braced exponent/subscript behavior remains unchanged. Regression test: apps/mobile/src/lib/math-format.test.ts fails without the fix and passes with it, adding x^23 and a_12 expectations alongside the existing braced cases.
